intro: Foreign currency revaluation and currency translation are distinct mechanisms in the custody chain. Revaluation restates monetary balances for ledger measurement; translation converts measured results into reporting or consolidation presentation.
doctrine_statement:
Revaluation changes measured ledger posture. Translation changes presentation posture.
Both require evidence, but they do not answer the same custody question.
primary_section_title: Separate remeasurement mechanics from presentation translation.
primary_section_body: AccountCcy treats revaluation as a State 05 ledger discipline tied to open monetary populations, rate sources, timing, and approval evidence.
Translation belongs to State 06 and State 07 presentation layers, where measured ledger results are converted for reporting, consolidation, and group visibility.
The distinction matters because misclassification can distort unrealized FX, equity translation effects, reporting bridges, and audit explanations.

pillar_sections.title: Where each mechanism sits in the CCY State Chain

steps.title: FX revaluation
steps.body: Restates open monetary balances in the ledger using governed rates.

steps.title: Remeasurement
steps.body: Measurement mechanics tied to functional and ledger currency context.

steps.title: Currency translation
steps.body: Converts measured results into reporting or consolidation presentation.

steps.title: Reporting currency
steps.body: Presentation layer where translation becomes visible.

pillar_sections.title: Evidence required to defend revaluation and translation
paragraphs: Revaluation evidence includes population listings, rate-source approval, run logs, exception review, and ledger posting support.
Translation evidence includes methodology memos, reporting packages, translation rates, equity movement support, consolidation roll-forwards, and bridge schedules.
Both require versioned artifacts. Neither should rely on a spreadsheet whose origin cannot be replayed.

pillar_sections.title: Revaluation and translation control questions
questions: Which balances are included in revaluation populations and why?
Which exchange-rate source governed the run and who approved overrides?
Where are unrealized and realized FX separated?
Which translation methodology applies to reporting or consolidation packages?
How are translation adjustments reconciled to equity or reporting bridges?
Can auditors reperformance-test both mechanisms independently?

graph_title: Revaluation and translation are adjacent but not interchangeable.
graph_intro: This page protects AccountCcy's terminology from one of the most common multi-currency accounting collapses.

cards.label: Ledger
cards.title: Revaluation operates on measured balances.
cards.body: It is tied to open monetary populations, rates, and ledger posting evidence.

cards.label: Reporting
cards.title: Translation operates on presentation.
cards.body: It converts measured results into reporting or group currency views.

cards.label: Audit
cards.title: Evidence must separate mechanics.
cards.body: Reviewers need to test revaluation and translation without blended narratives.

control_title: Separate run logs, rate evidence, and bridge schedules.
control_body: Keep revaluation evidence and translation evidence distinct while linking both to the same custody chain.
risk_title: FX explanations that sound right but test poorly.
risk_body: If revaluation and translation are blended, committees may approve narratives that cannot survive reperformance.

faq.question: Are revaluation and translation the same?
faq.answer: No. Revaluation restates ledger monetary balances; translation converts measured results into reporting or consolidation currency.

faq.question: Which state owns revaluation?
faq.answer: Primarily State 05 Ledger Currency, with dependencies from account currency and downstream reporting effects.
